primary_accounting_issue: Prise en compte du risque de défaut dans la mesure de la juste valeur d’un actif ou d’un passif selon IFRS 13

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I selected IFRS 13 because the question is about the fair value measurement basis itself, and IFRS 13 is the governing IFRS that defines fair value and sets the measurement framework; IFRS 9 only applies that framework in specific financial instrument contexts rather than governing the core measurement issue.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty, because IFRS 13 directly addresses how risk assumptions, including credit-related non-performance risk, enter fair value measurements for both assets and liabilities.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern whether and how market-participant credit and non-performance risk are reflected in fair value for assets and liabilities.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs confirm, in the financial liabilities context, that changes attributable to the liability’s own credit risk are part of fair value and provide supporting measurement guidance.

peripheral_authority.reason: These hedge accounting provisions are topically related to fair value changes but do not help identify peer approaches for whether counterparty default risk is included in fair value.
These paragraphs use fair value in agriculture but do not contribute to identifying the relevant fair value credit-risk approaches for assets and liabilities.

treatment_families.family: Fair value of an asset based on market participant assumptions about the asset’s risk characteristics
Fair value of a liability reflecting transfer assumptions and non-performance risk including own credit risk

approaches.label: Fair value of an asset
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: For an asset, fair value is measured using market participant assumptions about the asset, which includes relevant credit-related risk characteristics affecting the price a buyer would pay.

approaches.label: Fair value of a liability
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: For a liability, fair value explicitly reflects non-performance risk, including the issuer’s own credit risk, in the price to transfer the liability.
